meta.name: Marathon Performance
meta.subtitle: Sub-3:15 — 4:37/km for the full 42.2km
meta.description: A 32-week high-performance marathon plan adapted from the NextSplit v1 tracker for athletes targeting sub-3:15. Built on Pfitzinger and Douglas principles: high aerobic volume, two quality sessions per week, marathon-pace long run sections, and progressive overload with planned deload weeks.
meta.coach_notes: Sub-3:15 requires 4:37/km for 42.2km. This plan requires 90+ km per week at peak and assumes you are already running 60km/week consistently. The marathon-pace work is the most important element — specifically the Sunday long runs with extended MP sections. These train your body to run 4:37/km on fatigued legs, which is exactly what marathon day demands.

weeks.title: Base week 1
weeks.note: Opening week. Six running days. Establish your easy pace (5:35–6:00/km) and threshold feel. Do not rush adaptation.

sessions.det: 12km easy · 5:30–5:55/km — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

2km WU · 8x1km @ threshold (4:10–4:20/km) (2.5min rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
13km easy · 5:35–6:00/km — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

2km WU · 28min @ threshold (4:10–4:20/km) · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Goblet squat 2x12 · Romanian deadlift 2x12 · Reverse lunge 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first lower body strength session. Keep loads light — this week is about learning the movements. Lower body strength is the foundation of running economy.
13km easy · 8x100m strides — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
22km easy · 5:45–6:05/km — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 45min on runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30g+ protein. Your most important weekly nutrition window.

weeks.title: Base week 2
weeks.note: VO2max intervals introduced. These build the aerobic ceiling.

sessions.det: 13km easy · 5:30–5:55/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 6x1km @ 3:55–4:05/km (3min rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
13km easy · 5:35–6:00/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 2x16min @ threshold (2min rec) · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.
13km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
24km easy — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 45min on runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30g+ protein. Your most important weekly nutrition window.

weeks.title: Base week 3
weeks.note: Volume peak in first block.

sessions.det: 13km easy · 5:30–5:55/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
13km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
26km easy — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 45min on runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30g+ protein. Your most important weekly nutrition window.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

weeks.title: Race-specific 3
weeks.note: 38km peak long run — furthest of the plan.

sessions.det: 14km easy · 5:30–5:55/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · 4x3km @ 4:37/km (4min rec) · 2km CD — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.
13km easy · 5:35–6:00/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · 42min @ threshold · 2km CD — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
14km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

18km easy · last 20km @ 4:37/km — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 45min on runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30g+ protein. Your most important weekly nutrition window.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Fifth deload. The race is 11 weeks away.

sessions.det: 9km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
9km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
8km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
8km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
9km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
22km easy · Comfortable — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 45min on runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30g+ protein. Your most important weekly nutrition window.

weeks.note: Final peak block. Confidence at MP is high by now.

weeks.title: Taper prep
weeks.note: Volume reduces. Intensity maintained.

sessions.det: 11km easy · 5:30–5:55/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 4x2km @ 4:37/km (3min rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
11km easy · 5:35–6:00/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · 28min @ threshold · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 4x8 · Romanian deadlift 4x8 · Bulgarian split squat 4x8 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 4x8s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.
11km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

18km easy · last 8km @ 4:37/km — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 45min on runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30g+ protein. Your most important weekly nutrition window.

2km WU · 3x1km @ 4:37/km (90sec rec) · 2km CD — Sharp intervals close to race week keep your legs feeling quick without adding volume. The fitness is already built — this is a reminder, not a workout.

weeks.title: Race week — sub-3:15
weeks.note: Race week. The work is done. Trust it.

sessions.det: 3km easy · 4x100m strides at MP — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
3km easy · Very light — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Race: through 10km at 4:40, settle to 4:37/km, hold to 30km, build final 12km · Gel at 10/21/30/35km · 32 weeks of training is your foundation — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey, or bagel with peanut butter. 600ml water. Only foods eaten before every long run.
Gel with full cup of water. Same brand you trained with.
Gel with water at halfway. You should feel controlled here.
Gel with water. Race starts now.
Isotonic drink immediately. 30g protein within 30 minutes. Full meal within 2 hours.
